message:
chore(beads): archive 3 perf wins (WAL adapter, btree predecessor, mvcc gate)

Files three already-closed beads capturing the perf work that landed
in commits d9c410bb, b35f091c, and f2707d1a so the reasoning and
benchmark numbers survive outside commit-message prose:

- bd-10jx8 (perf/wal_adapter): documents the drop of the per-read
  frame_buf[HEADER..].to_vec() allocation in
  WalBackendAdapter::read_page_pinned, replaced with an in-place
  copy_within + truncate. One page-sized allocation + free removed
  per pinned WAL read on the hot path for concurrent readers.

- bd-ah597 (perf/btree): documents the predecessor_idx rowid-only
  parse via CellRef::parse_leaf_table_rowid (two varints) instead of
  parse_cell_at + full CellRef::parse + CellSlotCache round-trip.
  Microbench bench_parse_leaf_table_rowid_vs_full_parse shows
  3.216ns vs 6.474ns = 2.01× on the parse itself plus ~4-8ns saved
  per call from skipping the cache miss path (the bd-9e3xf audit
  showed 99% MISS_INVALIDATED on DELETE). Same "caller only needs one
  field" pattern as 4b061dcc (child_page_at) and 385591f8
  (replace_separator).

- bd-z9kx0 (perf/mvcc): documents gating the visibility_ranges
  side-index off the publish() + GC hot paths (default off via
  AtomicBool, matching the bc4fa6b5 pattern). Microbench
  2582 → 2364 ns/publish (-8.5%, n=9, sign consistent).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
